column6:
Total 12 qns in OA round 
2 python coding questions ( We were only allowed to code in either python or c++ since they were looking for python proficiency). 5-8 aptitude qns and rest pseudo code ig.
column7: GD round was there. Topic was "Impact of AI on our jobs". Try to speak more on the topic rather than going out of scope and attend Mouna mam's gd classes at least. It helped a lot.
column8:
Tip: Don't write anything on resume which you can't answer about and be honest 
Started with normal intro. They asked if I can code in python. I did my DSA in Java but I knew python too. I told them that my main stack is java but I can code in python and I can learn more of required. They gave me 2 DSA(easy to medium level) which I coded. Then asked questions about java like inheritance, oops, constructors and destructors and other basic questions. Then they asked me 2 aptitude questions. Asked about features and advantages of java. The interviewer was also typing something ( maybe checking my GitHub and leetcode). I had also experience with open source contributions so we discussed about that too. Overall 40-50 min interview. Try being confident about your stack and whatever you write on resume you should be able to justify that you know it.
